8c67f5090533.title: Get your menu online — 77% of diners check before visiting
8c67f5090533.description: No online menu found. Most diners check the menu before committing. Without one, you lose the customer at the decision point.
8c67f5090533.management_implication: Every day without a menu is a percentage of potential customers lost.

fb79d1fbc122.title: Establish basic web presence — you don't exist online beyond Google
fb79d1fbc122.description: No website, Facebook, or Instagram detected. Even a basic page improves discoverability and gives customers a reason to trust you before visiting.

f800d9bb3264.title: Fix the digital shopfront — you're losing walk-ins
f800d9bb3264.description: You deliver a strong experience but your online presence doesn't make it easy to act on. Can a new customer confirm your hours, see your menu, and book — all within 60 seconds on their phone? If not, that's your fix list.
f800d9bb3264.management_implication: Interest without conversion is vanity traffic.

dd9eeb855b64.title: Strong rating masks weak competitive position
dd9eeb855b64.description: Your 4.6/5 Google rating feels dominant but you sit below the local median (P0.0). Peer venues achieve similar or better ratings — in this market, 4.5 is table stakes, not a differentiator. Don't mistake the number for an advantage.
dd9eeb855b64.management_implication: The rating looks good in isolation but not in context.

94d5a0f90d54.title: Ranked #2 of 2 locally — peers are ahead
94d5a0f90d54.description: At percentile 0.0, customers in this area have higher-rated alternatives. This isn't a crisis but it means you're competing on convenience rather than reputation.

c5834105bce5.title: Protect the 4.6/5 rating — it took years to build
c5834105bce5.description: With 441 reviews at 4.6/5, your discovery advantage is substantial. Protect it by responding to every review within 48 hours and prompting satisfied tables for feedback.
